title: Research Assistant (Information Systems)
description:
We are looking for a Research Assistant to support the division’s mission of advancing impactful research by working closely with the Principal Investigator and lab members on one or more ongoing projects. In this role, he/she contributes directly to generating high-quality insights that advance knowledge in Information Systems and related domains. The Research Assistant is expected to bring experience or a strong interest in at least one of the division’s core research areas, helping drive studies that inform practice, shape academic discourse, and support ITOM’s broader goal of creating research with meaningful societal impact.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Research support: Conduct literature reviews, assist in project design, and collect, clean, and analyze primary and secondary data.

  • Data analysis: Utilize statistical software like STATA or R, depending on the project's needs.

  • Project administration: Help with project management tasks such as seeking ethics approvals, tracking budgets, and preparing progress reports.

  • Lab work and Fieldwork: Coordinate research laboratory sessions and fieldwork, which may involve conducting experiments, surveys, interviews, or interventions, and managing data quality.

  • Dissemination: Contribute to the writing of research papers, reports, policy briefs, and presentations for various audiences.

  • Event organization: Assist in organizing research seminars, conferences, and other events.

Requirements:

  • Bachelor’s in information systems or related field

  • At least 3 years of research experience in laboratory settings

  • Familiarity with neuroscience techniques (e.g., eye-tracking)

  • Good written and oral communication skills

  • Proficiency in data management, statistical analyses

  • Competent in information visualization and interaction design

  • Interpersonal skill (e.g., Ability to work independently / develop solutions under strict timelines, meticulous and eye for details / excellent organizational / time management skills)

We regret to inform that only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

postedCompany.description:
A research-intensive public university, Nanyang Technological University, Singapore (NTU Singapore) has 33,000 undergraduate and postgraduate students in the Engineering, Business, Science, Humanities, Arts, & Social Sciences, and Graduate colleges. It also has a medical school, the Lee Kong Chian School of Medicine, established jointly with Imperial College London.


NTU is also home to world-class autonomous institutes – the National Institute of Education, S Rajaratnam School of International Studies, Earth Observatory of Singapore, and Singapore Centre for Environmental Life Sciences Engineering – and various leading research centres such as the Nanyang Environment & Water Research Institute (NEWRI) and Energy Research Institute @ NTU (ERI@N).


Ranked amongst the world’s top universities by QS, NTU has also been named the world’s top young university for the past seven years. The University’s main campus is frequently listed among the Top 15 most beautiful university campuses in the world and has 57 Green Mark-certified (equivalent to LEED-certified) buildings, of which 95% are certified Green Mark Platinum. Apart from its main campus, NTU also has a campus in Novena, Singapore’s healthcare district.


Under the NTU Smart Campus vision, the University harnesses the power of digital technology and tech-enabled solutions to support better learning and living experiences, the discovery of new knowledge, and the sustainability of resources.


For more information, visit www.ntu.edu.sg
postedCompany.name: NANYANG TECHNOLOGICAL UNIVERSITY
